daily.topThemeTitle: Who Gets In, on Whose Terms
daily.topThemeBody: Access is contested at both ends: Inside Higher Ed charts the rise of the nontraditional student and business officers pressing for structural change over storm-weathering, while Brookings warns the Global South must be a design partner rather than a recipient and that scaling edtech in Asia prizes feasibility over evidence. EdSurge finds rural districts lack AI training, and ISTELive panelists urge leaders to defend E-Rate.

themes.title: Who Gets In, on Whose Terms

heroWeek.themeBody: ISTELive 26 keeps generating skills coverage: EdTech Magazine and EdSurge unpack the six-role "AI-Ready Graduate" and a "relational intelligence" recap, experts urge leaders to defend E-Rate broadband funding, Inside Higher Ed's RAISE US targets workforce reskilling, and 1EdTech advances research on scaling digital credentials.
heroWeek.themeProp: graduation cap over a small network of nodes
